name: Canva Pro
price_basis: Pro at US$144 billed yearly, normalized monthly, checked 2026-07
verdict_reason: TRIM: the editor rides free. You are paying $144 a year for AI features your server already has.
what_zo_builds: Zo generates and edits images on demand, resizes and converts formats, strips backgrounds, and files brand assets in a /brand folder with your colors, fonts, and logo variants so every asset request starts from the same kit. The Canva editor itself stays in your life on the free plan; what leaves is the subscription that mostly bills you for AI credits and premium stock you rarely open.
dont_kill_if: A team shares brand kits and scheduled social content through your Pro seat, or you pull premium stock weekly. Licensed stock at volume is cheaper bundled than bought per image.
build_brief: Goal: downgrade Canva to the free plan and route design work through my own tools. Create /brand with logo files, hex colors, and font names. Test Zo on my five most common asset types: social cards, thumbnails, banners, simple edits, background removal. Save a reusable prompt template for each. Export anything stored only in Canva. Run two weeks on the free editor plus Zo for generation. Done when no asset request needed Pro and the plan is downgraded.
